name: SafePay Ransomware - File Encrypted with .safepay Extension
description: Detects creation of files with the .safepay extension, the hallmark encryption artifact of SafePay ransomware. SafePay appends .safepay to the original filename after encrypting file contents. Any process creating .safepay files on an enterprise endpoint should be treated as a confirmed ransomware infection requiring immediate isolation. This pattern is specific to the SafePay ransomware family and should not generate false positives in production environments.
query: file where event.action == "creation" and file.name like~ "*.safepay"
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware Ransom Note Created in User Directory
description: Detects creation of text or HTML files matching common ransomware ransom note naming patterns in user-accessible directories. SafePay drops 'readme_safepay.txt' while other ransomware families use HOW_TO_DECRYPT, RECOVER_FILES, FILES_ENCRYPTED, and similar naming. This detection targets the ransom note drop behavior rather than any specific family, making it effective against a broad range of ransomware variants. Ransom notes are typically the final artifact written after encryption completes, making this a high-confidence late-stage indicator.
query:
file where event.action == "creation" and
  file.path like~ "*\\Users\\*" and
  (
    file.name like~ "readme_safepay*" or
    file.name like~ "*RECOVER_FILES*" or
    file.name like~ "*HOW_TO_DECRYPT*" or
    file.name like~ "*DECRYPT_INSTRUCTIONS*" or
    file.name like~ "*FILES_ENCRYPTED*" or
    file.name like~ "*YOUR_FILES*"
  ) and
  file.extension in ("txt", "html", "hta")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Archive Utility Executing from Non-Standard Path for Data Staging
description: Detects WinRAR or rar.exe executing outside of standard Program Files installation paths with command-line arguments consistent with bulk recursive data collection: the 'a' (add) command combined with recursive flag (-r) and optional flags like -ep1 (exclude base folder) or -m (compression level). Ransomware operators stage data by archiving victim file collections prior to or during encryption. Executing the archiver from a temporary or user-writable path indicates the binary was dropped rather than being a pre-installed application. Maps to T1560.001 (Archive Collected Data: Archive via Utility).
query:
process where event.action == "start" and
  process.name in~ ("WinRAR.exe", "rar.exe") and
  not process.executable like~ "*\\Program Files\\WinRAR\\*" and
  not process.executable like~ "*\\Program Files (x86)\\WinRAR\\*" and
  process.args : " a " and
  process.command_line like~ "*-r*"
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware Kill Chain - Encryption Followed by Ransom Note Drop
description: Sequence detection for a high-confidence ransomware kill chain: a process first creates files with a suspicious double-extension pattern (original.docx.safepay or similar ransomware extension), then within 10 minutes the same host sees a ransom note file created. This two-event sequence reliably distinguishes active ransomware from isolated file creation events. The sequence correlates by host.id to catch cases where a parent process spawns children for each phase. Covers SafePay and other families following the encrypt-then-notify pattern.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=10m
  [file where event.action == "creation" and
   file.name like~ "*.safepay" or
   file.name regex~ ".*\\.(docx?|xlsx?|pdf|csv|txt|ppt[x]?|sql|bak)\\.[a-z0-9]{3,12}$"]
  [file where event.action == "creation" and
   file.extension in ("txt", "html", "hta") and
   (
     file.name like~ "*readme*" or
     file.name like~ "*ransom*" or
     file.name like~ "*decrypt*" or
     file.name like~ "*recover*"
   )]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Ransomware Pre-Encryption Data Staging - Archive Utility Preceded by Bulk File Creation
description: Sequence detection for pre-encryption data staging: within 30 minutes on the same host, a process first creates more than 50 files in user-accessible directories, then an archive utility (WinRAR, rar.exe, 7z.exe) executes. This sequence matches the SafePay and similar ransomware operators' pattern of generating or collecting victim files then compressing them for exfiltration before or alongside encryption. The two-stage sequence provides higher confidence than either indicator alone. Maps to T1005 (Data from Local System) and T1560.001 (Archive Collected Data).
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=30m
  [file where event.action == "creation" and
   file.path like~ "*\\Users\\*" and
   file.extension in ("docx", "doc", "xlsx", "xls", "pdf", "txt", "csv", "sql", "bak", "ppt", "pptx")] with runs=50
  [process where event.action == "start" and
   process.name in~ ("WinRAR.exe", "rar.exe", "7z.exe", "7za.exe") and
   process.command_line like~ "* a *"]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
